issue_title: Chat UI header shows “Connected” when `/api/health` reports a degraded stack
issue_body: ## Summary The embedded web chat UI (`crates/genie-core/src/chat_ui.html`) shows a green status dot and the label **Connected** whenever `/api/health` returns HTTP 200, without reading `status` or `llm` from the JSON body. When genie-core is up but the LLM backend is offline (or connectivity is degraded), the API correctly returns `"status": "degraded"` and `"llm": "offline"`, yet the UI still tells the user the system is fully connected. ## Duplicate check Searched open and closed issues and pull requests on `GeniePod/genie-claw` (May 2026). **No duplicate found** for this UI status-indicator bug. | Result | Notes | | --- | --- | | No open issue | Queries for `checkHealth`, `status-dot`, `degraded connected`, `misleading health`, `llm offline ui` returned no matches. | | [#73](https://github.com/GeniePod/genie-claw/issues/73) (closed) | Different problem: core `/api/health` reported `llm: connected` while `/api/chat` failed against `genie-ai-runtime` (integration/adapter bug, fixed in PR #74). | | [PR #81](https://github.com/GeniePod/genie-claw/pull/81) (merged) | Animated writing indicator only; did not change `checkHealth()`. | | [PR #84](https://github.com/GeniePod/genie-claw/pull/84) (merged) | Port 3080 dashboard services table; not the port 3000 embedded chat UI header. | This issue is about the **port 3000 chat UI ignoring health fields**, not about health lying to operators (as in #73). ## Current behavior 1. Start genie-core with the LLM backend stopped or unreachable (for example `genie-llm.service` down, or no runtime on the configured URL). 2. Confirm the API reports degradation: ```bash curl -s http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/health | jq '{status, llm, llm_backend}' ``` Example when the LLM is down: ```json { "status": "degraded", "llm": "offline", "llm_backend": "genie-ai-runtime" } ``` 3. Open the chat UI in a browser (same host/port as genie-core, typically `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`). 4. Observe the header status: - Green dot (`.dot`, not `.dot.offline`) - Text: **Connected** 5. Sending a message fails or returns an error in the chat pane, while the header still shows **Connected**. `checkHealth()` runs on load and every 10 seconds; it never inspects `data.status` or `data.llm`: ```javascript async function checkHealth() { try { const r = await fetch('/api/health'); const data = await r.json(); statusDot.className = 'dot'; statusText.textContent = 'Connected'; } catch { statusDot.className = 'dot offline'; statusText.textContent = 'Offline'; } } ``` ## Expected behavior - **Core unreachable** (fetch error or non-2xx): red dot, label such as **Offline**. - **`status: "ok"` and `llm: "connected"`**: green dot, **Connected** (or **Ready**). - **`status: "degraded"` and/or `llm: "offline"`**: warning state (for example amber dot), label such as **Degraded** or **LLM offline**, so users know chat may not work before they send a message. - Optionally surface connectivity degradation when `connectivity.state` is not `disabled` / `ready` (same semantics as `overall_health_status()` in `crates/genie-core/src/server.rs`). Align with operator docs: [doc/deployment-and-ops.md](https://github.com/GeniePod/genie-claw/blob/main/doc/deployment-and-ops.md) lists “Chat UI loads but no answers” → check `/api/health`; the UI should reflect that health signal. ## Context - Health endpoint: `GET /api/health` in `crates/genie-core/src/server.rs` sets `status` via `overall_health_status()` and `llm` to `"connected"` or `"offline"`. - The chat UI is served from the same binary (`chat_ui.html` embedded in genie-core) and is the primary local operator surface on port 3000. - `genie-ctl health` already prints separate lines for core reachability and LLM state; only the web UI header is misleading today. - CSS today only defines `.dot` (green) and `.dot.offline` (red); a third “degraded/warning” style may be needed. ## Acceptance criteria - With LLM up and `status: "ok"`, header shows a positive connected state (green dot). - With genie-core up but `llm: "offline"` (and `status: "degraded"`), header does **not** show **Connected**; it shows a degraded/warning state and text that indicates the LLM is unavailable. - With genie-core down or `/api/health` unreachable, header shows **Offline** (red dot). - Status updates on the existing 10s poll without a full page reload. - Add a regression test in `genie-core` (for example assert `chat_ui.html` contains logic that reads `status` or `llm` from the health response, similar to `chat_ui_uses_animated_writing_indicator` in `tool_dispatch_test.rs`). ## Suggested fix (for implementers) - Parse `data.status` and `data.llm` in `checkHealth()`. - Map to three UI states: offline / degraded / ok. - Add `.dot.degraded` (or similar) using an existing or new CSS variable (for example amber) between green and red.
